runs.content:
J.B. Hunt (JBHT) picked up in Portfolio 1 at the previous close ($298.41) — beat Q2 EPS by ~12% and revenue by ~9.5% on ~2.47× average volume, chosen over the deepening chip-sector selloff dominating today's tape. Two exits: XOM hit its 60-day max hold and closed at −7.48%; PENG stopped out at −15.93% on the chip weakness.

Abbott (ABT) entered Portfolio 2 at today's open (+2.20% gap) after raising FY26 adjusted EPS guidance on ~2.47× average volume. Two stop-outs today: ABSI (−25.45%, post-initiation pullback) and PENG (−20.13%, chip-sector selloff) — both full-position exits with no T1/T2 previously triggered.

Vertex Pharmaceuticals (VRTX) picked up in Portfolio 1 at the previous close ($477.08) — up ~24% over the trailing month on the $10B Crinetics Pharmaceuticals acquisition and a UBS price-target hike to $585, a momentum entry rather than another earnings-day gap-chase (Strategy D was used yesterday on ASML). No exits today; the broad chip-sector selloff eased MU (+9.74%→+0.94%) and AMAT (+30.02%→+26.47%), both with T1/T2 already banked.

First Horizon (FHN) entered Portfolio 2 at today's open (−0.9% gap) after a clean Q2 earnings beat (EPS $0.54 vs $0.53 est., net income +12% YoY) drove ~3.0× average volume on Jul 15 — a cleaner signal than BlackRock's own Q2 beat, which only ran ~1.9× average volume, just under the 2× threshold.
